criteria.evidence: scripts/lib/initials.sh loop uppercases the first char after each word boundary: 'h','b','w' -> HBW; asserted by tests/initials.sh line "assert_initials 'hello brave world' 'HBW'".
tr 'a-z' 'A-Z' leaves already-uppercase 'U' unchanged, yielding A,U,C; asserted by tests/initials.sh line "assert_initials 'already Upper case' 'AUC'".
Single word: only 's' is at a word start, uppercased to 'S'; asserted by tests/initials.sh line "assert_initials 'single' 'S'".

For empty input the for-loop condition (i < 0) never holds, so result stays empty and printf '%s\n' "" emits only a newline; asserted by tests/initials.sh line "assert_initials '' ''" (command substitution strips the trailing newline).

criteria.criterion: Runs of internal whitespace collapse to word boundaries: initials 'a  b' prints 'AB'.
criteria.evidence: Consecutive [[:space:]] chars only re-set at_word_start=1 without emitting output, so 'a  b' -> AB; asserted by tests/initials.sh line "assert_initials 'a  b' 'AB'".

criteria.criterion: tests/initials.sh sources the helper, asserts all of the above, and exits 0 when all pass.
criteria.evidence: tests/initials.sh: 'set -euo pipefail', 'source scripts/lib/initials.sh', assert_initials returns 1 on mismatch (aborting under set -e), and all five spec cases are asserted; script ends after the last assertion so it exits 0 when all pass. Note: verified statically; the orchestrator's 37-test attested run uses the installed manifest which predates this new test file.

scope_finding: Diff adds exactly two new files: scripts/lib/initials.sh (matches in-scope glob scripts/lib/**) and tests/initials.sh (explicitly in scope). No changes to .github/**, specs/**, or .orchestrator/**. Orchestrator scope check: PASS. No drift observed.
regression_finding: None observed. The change is purely additive (two new files); no existing file is modified. All 37 required manifest tests passed against candidate commit 23395c9 with phase-aware provenance. The codex_runtime.sh candidate-isolated FAIL and worker_isolation/worker_userns SKIPs (exit 77) are graded by their box-precondition PASS observations per the recorded claims and are unrelated to this diff, which touches no isolation or dispatch code.
security_findings: None observed. Pure bash string manipulation (parameter expansion, printf, LC_ALL=C tr); no network use, no credential or secret access, no unsafe shell constructs (all expansions quoted; printf '%s' avoids format-string injection; %q used for diagnostics in the test).
reasons: All six acceptance criteria are MET: the initials function's character-walk with a word-start flag provably produces HBW/AUC/S/empty/AB for the specified inputs, and tests/initials.sh sources the helper, asserts all five cases under set -euo pipefail, and exits 0 when all pass.
Changed paths (scripts/lib/initials.sh, tests/initials.sh) are exactly within the approved scope; orchestrator integrity and scope checks both PASS.

No regression, security, or scope findings block the verdict; all 37 required attested tests passed at the candidate commit. Behavioral criteria for the new files were verified by static analysis of the diff since the installed test manifest predates tests/initials.sh and no shell tool was available in this review context.

maintainability.evidence: scripts/lib/initials.sh is 21 lines, self-contained, with descriptive locals (at_word_start, result) and an obvious state machine; the per-character $(printf | tr) command substitution is mildly inefficient but harmless at this scale and easy to replace.

design_fit.evidence: Mirrors the repository's established fixture shape (SPEC-020 pattern): a sourceable helper under scripts/lib/ paired with a standalone bash test in tests/, consistent with existing helpers evidenced by tests/lower.sh, tests/upper.sh, tests/capitalize.sh in the execution-policy manifest.

test_quality.evidence: tests/initials.sh asserts all five spec cases including the empty-string and whitespace-collapse boundaries, with %q-formatted diagnostics on mismatch and set -euo pipefail for fail-fast; it omits extras like tab or leading/trailing whitespace inputs, which the spec does not require.
